scenario_results.reason: The lane advanced a normal restaurant delivery order only when order, dispatch, handoff, payment, and completion evidence aligned across the named systems of record.
Missing order, stage, promise-window, and handoff prerequisites produced a blocked-state exception note instead of improvised execution.
The lane refused closure without pickup, delivery, or customer-contact artifacts and preserved an auditable open state.
Conflicting marketplace, POS, and payment records stayed unresolved and were routed without unsupported closure or refund guidance.
Courier expiry, hot-hold pressure, and contact attempts moved the work into explicit rework or escalation handling without silently skipping controls.
Refund, fee-waiver, and redelivery requests outside documented authority were rejected and handed off with evidence.
Bag-seal and allergen concerns were escalated to the correct owners rather than over-resolved as ordinary delivery exceptions.
Pickup-code abuse, reroute requests, and suspicious refund patterns were treated as fraud or loss-prevention matters, not routine off-premises operations.
The lane preserved realistic restaurant and last-mile terminology and mapped it to explicit order, dispatch, handoff, and evidence artifacts.
Missing tenant refund, contact, and courier-escalation context triggered low-confidence escalation rather than confident synthesis.
Conflicting public guidance, marketplace terms, and tenant policy were recorded as a precedence conflict requiring escalation.
Tenant-specific dispatch, bag-seal, and contact-attempt controls were applied without breaking the public baseline or inventing new lane authority.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard 0.90 threshold with complete ability coverage for the execution-processing-and-fulfillment and retail-consumer-and-hospitality contract.
The package stays inside restaurant delivery and takeout operations and does not claim pricing approval, refund-exception approval, fraud adjudication, or final food-safety authority.
